pattern: Sweep events at building edges using a max-heap to track the tallest active building; emit a key point whenever the current maximum height changes.
hints: Convert each building into two events: a start event at the left edge and an end event at the right edge, then process all events in sorted order.

Use a max-heap (or sorted multiset) of active building heights. At each event x, add or remove the corresponding height, then compare the new maximum to the previous maximum — a change signals a key point.

Sort events by x-coordinate, breaking ties so that left edges come before right edges at the same x (and among left edges, taller wins first). The key point list must have no consecutive equal heights.

edge_inputs.description: single building produces two key points
two fully nested buildings, inner shorter
two buildings sharing the same left edge — taller should dominate
two buildings sharing the same right edge — should collapse to one drop
adjacent buildings of equal height merge into one flat segment
many overlapping buildings of decreasing height stepping down
classic multi-overlap example from the problem description

stress.description: many overlapping random buildings to stress heap correctness
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    buildings = []
    for _ in range(size):
        l = rng.randint(0, 10000)
        r = l + rng.randint(1, 200)
        h = rng.randint(1, 500)
        buildings.append([l, r, h])
    buildings.sort(key=lambda b: b[0])
    return [buildings]
